company: Shanghai United Imaging Healthcare Co., Ltd.
intended_use: uDR Aurora CX is intended to acquire X-ray images of the human body by a qualified technician, examples include acquiring two-dimensional X-ray images of the skull, spinal column, chest, abdomen, extr
intended_use_full: uDR Aurora CX is intended to acquire X-ray images of the human body by a qualified technician, examples include acquiring two-dimensional X-ray images of the skull, spinal column, chest, abdomen, extremities, limbs and trunk. The visualization of such anatomical structures provide visual evidence to radiologists and clinicians in making diagnostic decisions. This device is not intended for mammography.
target_condition: Chest, spinal, and lower-extremity radiography positioning and image quality assessment
ground_truth: For uVision: clinical expert evaluation of automatically set positioning and FOV. For uAid: clinician assessment of chest image quality using four criteria; data sourced from cooperative hospitals and stored in DICOM format.
other_metrics: uVision: 95% compliance rate for automatically set system position and field size meeting clinical technician criteria. uAid: Sensitivity and specificity exceed 0.9 for foreign body detection, lung field intactness, and scapula assessment; average processing time 1.359 seconds (max 2 seconds); maximum memory occupation not more than 2GB.
